controls.title: Security management process
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations.

controls.title: Risk analysis
controls.description: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of electronic protected health information held by the covered entity or business associate.

controls.title: Risk management
controls.description: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with the Security Rule.

controls.title: Sanction policy
controls.description: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the security policies and procedures of the covered entity or business associate.

controls.title: Information system activity review
controls.description: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

controls.title: Assigned security responsibility
controls.description: Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by the Security Rule for the covered entity or business associate.

controls.title: Workforce security
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of its workforce have appropriate access to electronic protected health information and to prevent those workforce members who do not have access from obtaining access.

controls.title: Authorization and/or supervision
controls.description: Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members who work with electronic protected health information or in locations where it might be accessed.

controls.title: Workforce clearance procedure
controls.description: Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to electronic protected health information is appropriate.

controls.title: Termination procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures for terminating access to electronic protected health information when the employment of, or other arrangement with, a workforce member ends.

controls.title: Information access management
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of the Privacy Rule.

controls.title: Isolating health care clearinghouse functions
controls.description: If a health care clearinghouse is part of a larger organization, the clearinghouse must implement policies and procedures that protect the electronic protected health information of the clearinghouse from unauthorized access by the larger organization.

controls.title: Access authorization
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for granting access to electronic protected health information, for example, through access to a workstation, transaction, program, process, or other mechanism.

controls.title: Access establishment and modification
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that, based upon the covered entity's or the business associate's access authorization policies, establish, document, review, and modify a user's right of access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process.

controls.title: Security awareness and training
controls.description: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of its workforce, including management.

controls.title: Security reminders
controls.description: Provide periodic security updates to all members of the workforce.

controls.title: Protection from malicious software
controls.description: Implement procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.

controls.title: Log-in monitoring
controls.description: Implement procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies.

controls.title: Password management
controls.description: Implement procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords.

controls.title: Security incident procedures
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents.

controls.title: Response and reporting
controls.description: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents; mitigate, to the extent practicable, harmful effects of security incidents that are known to the covered entity or business associate; and document security incidents and their outcomes.

controls.title: Contingency plan
controls.description: Establish and implement as needed policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence that damages systems that contain electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Data backup plan
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Disaster recovery plan
controls.description: Establish and implement as needed procedures to restore any loss of data.

controls.title: Emergency mode operation plan
controls.description: Establish and implement as needed procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes for protection of the security of electronic protected health information while operating in emergency mode.

controls.title: Testing and revision procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans.

controls.title: Applications and data criticality analysis
controls.description: Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data in support of other contingency plan components.

controls.title: Evaluation
controls.description: Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation, based initially upon the standards implemented under this rule and, subsequently, in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Business associate contracts and other arrangements
controls.description: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit electronic protected health information on the covered entity's behalf only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances that the business associate will appropriately safeguard the information.

controls.title: Written contract or other arrangement
controls.description: Document the satisfactory assurances required by the business associate contracts through a written contract or other arrangement with the business associate that meets the applicable requirements.

controls.title: Facility access controls
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to its electronic information systems and the facility or facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed.

controls.title: Contingency operations
controls.description: Establish and implement as needed procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan in the event of an emergency.

controls.title: Facility security plan
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and the equipment therein from unauthorized physical access, tampering, and theft.

controls.title: Access control and validation procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities based on their role or function, including visitor control, and control of access to software programs for testing and revision.

controls.title: Maintenance records
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to document repairs and modifications to the physical components of a facility which are related to security, such as hardware, walls, doors, and locks.

controls.title: Workstation use
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation or class of workstation that can access electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Workstation security
controls.description: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access electronic protected health information, to restrict access to authorized users.

controls.title: Device and media controls
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain electronic protected health information into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.

controls.title: Disposal
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of electronic protected health information, and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored.

controls.title: Media re-use
controls.description: Implement procedures for removal of electronic protected health information from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use.

controls.title: Accountability
controls.description: Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media and any person responsible therefore.

controls.title: Data backup and storage
controls.description: Create a retrievable, exact copy of electronic protected health information, when needed, before movement of equipment.

controls.title: Access control
controls.description: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain electronic protected health information to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights as specified in the information access management standard.

controls.title: Unique user identification
controls.description: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.

controls.title: Emergency access procedure
controls.description: Establish and implement as needed procedures for obtaining necessary electronic protected health information during an emergency.

controls.title: Automatic logoff
controls.description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.

controls.title: Encryption and decryption
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Audit controls
controls.description: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Integrity
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to protect electronic protected health information from improper alteration or destruction.

controls.title: Mechanism to authenticate electronic protected health information
controls.description: Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that electronic protected health information has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.

controls.title: Person or entity authentication
controls.description: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

controls.title: Transmission security
controls.description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

controls.title: Integrity controls
controls.description: Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted electronic protected health information is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.

controls.title: Encryption
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt electronic protected health information whenever deemed appropriate.

controls.title: Business associate contracts or other arrangements
controls.description: The contract or other arrangement between the covered entity and its business associate must meet the requirements of the Security Rule, including satisfactory assurances that the business associate will appropriately safeguard electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Business associate contract requirements
controls.description: The contract must provide that the business associate will implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards that reasonably and appropriately protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Reporting of security incidents by business associate
controls.description: The contract must provide that the business associate will report to the covered entity any security incident of which it becomes aware, including breaches of unsecured protected health information.

controls.title: Requirements for group health plans
controls.description: Except when the only electronic protected health information disclosed to a plan sponsor is disclosed pursuant to authorized enrollment or disenrollment functions, the plan documents of the group health plan must be amended to incorporate provisions to require the plan sponsor to implement reasonable and appropriate security measures.

controls.title: Plan document amendment — safeguard implementation
controls.description: The plan documents of the group health plan must require the plan sponsor to implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards that reasonably and appropriately protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the electronic protected health information that it creates, receives, maintains, or transmits on behalf of the group health plan.

controls.title: Plan document amendment — adequate separation
controls.description: The plan documents must require the plan sponsor to ensure that adequate separation between the group health plan and the plan sponsor is supported by reasonable and appropriate security measures.

controls.title: Plan document amendment — agent safeguards
controls.description: The plan documents must require the plan sponsor to ensure that any agent to whom it provides electronic protected health information agrees to implement reasonable and appropriate security measures to protect the information.

controls.title: Plan document amendment — reporting security incidents
controls.description: The plan documents must require the plan sponsor to report to the group health plan any security incident of which it becomes aware.

controls.title: Policies and procedures
controls.description: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards, implementation specifications, or other requirements of the Security Rule, taking into account the size, complexity, and capabilities of the covered entity or business associate.

controls.title: Documentation
controls.description: Maintain the policies and procedures implemented to comply with the Security Rule in written (which may be electronic) form; and if an action, activity or assessment is required by the Security Rule to be documented, maintain a written (which may be electronic) record of the action, activity, or assessment.

controls.title: Time limit on documentation retention
controls.description: Retain the documentation required for 6 years from the date of its creation or the date when it last was in effect, whichever is later.

controls.title: Availability of documentation
controls.description: Make documentation available to those persons responsible for implementing the procedures to which the documentation pertains.

controls.title: Updates to documentation
controls.description: Review documentation periodically, and update as needed, in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of the electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Uses and disclosures of protected health information — general rules
controls.description: A covered entity or business associate may not use or disclose protected health information, except as permitted or required by the Privacy Rule.

controls.title: Minimum necessary standard
controls.description: When using or disclosing protected health information or when requesting protected health information from another covered entity or business associate, a covered entity or business associate must make reasonable efforts to limit protected health information to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose of the use, disclosure, or request.

controls.title: Uses and disclosures — organizational requirements
controls.description: A covered entity that is a hybrid entity or an affiliated covered entity must comply with the applicable organizational requirements to ensure that protected health information is properly managed within and across organizational boundaries.

controls.title: Uses and disclosures for which an authorization is required
controls.description: Except as otherwise permitted or required, a covered entity may not use or disclose protected health information without an authorization that is valid under this section. A valid authorization must contain specific core elements and required statements.

controls.title: Uses and disclosures requiring an opportunity for the individual to agree or to object
controls.description: A covered entity may use or disclose protected health information, provided that the individual is informed in advance of the use or disclosure and has the opportunity to agree to or prohibit or restrict the use or disclosure.

controls.title: Uses and disclosures — required by law
controls.description: A covered entity may use or disclose protected health information to the extent that such use or disclosure is required by law and the use or disclosure complies with and is limited to the relevant requirements of such law.

controls.title: Uses and disclosures for public health activities
controls.description: A covered entity may disclose protected health information for public health activities and purposes to a public health authority that is authorized by law to collect or receive such information for the purpose of preventing or controlling disease, injury, or disability.

controls.title: Minimum necessary requirements
controls.description: A covered entity must identify those persons or classes of persons in its workforce who need access to protected health information to carry out their duties, the category or categories of protected health information to which access is needed, and any conditions appropriate to such access.

controls.title: Notice of privacy practices — general requirement
controls.description: A covered entity must provide a notice of its privacy practices that is written in plain language, describing how the covered entity may use and disclose protected health information and the individual's rights and the covered entity's legal duties with respect to protected health information.

controls.title: Notice of privacy practices — content requirements
controls.description: The notice must contain a header stating 'THIS NOTICE DESCRIBES HOW MEDICAL INFORMATION ABOUT YOU MAY BE USED AND DISCLOSED AND HOW YOU CAN GET ACCESS TO THIS INFORMATION,' and must describe uses and disclosures, individual rights, and covered entity duties.

controls.title: Rights to request privacy protection for protected health information
controls.description: A covered entity must permit an individual to request that the covered entity restrict uses or disclosures of protected health information about the individual to carry out treatment, payment, or health care operations, and disclosures to persons involved in the individual's health care.

controls.title: Access of individuals to protected health information
controls.description: An individual has a right of access to inspect and obtain a copy of protected health information about the individual in a designated record set, for as long as the protected health information is maintained in the designated record set, with limited exceptions.

controls.title: Provision of access — form and format
controls.description: The covered entity must provide the individual with access to the protected health information in the form and format requested by the individual, if it is readily producible in such form and format; or, if not, in a readable hard copy form or such other form and format as agreed to by the covered entity and the individual.

controls.title: Amendment of protected health information
controls.description: An individual has the right to have a covered entity amend protected health information or a record about the individual in a designated record set for as long as the protected health information is maintained in the designated record set.

controls.title: Accounting of disclosures of protected health information
controls.description: An individual has a right to receive an accounting of disclosures of protected health information made by a covered entity in the six years prior to the date on which the accounting is requested, except for disclosures for treatment, payment, and health care operations.

controls.title: Administrative requirements — personnel designations
controls.description: A covered entity must designate a privacy official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures of the entity and a contact person or office responsible for receiving complaints.

controls.title: Administrative requirements — training
controls.description: A covered entity must train all members of its workforce on the policies and procedures with respect to protected health information as necessary and appropriate for the members of the workforce to carry out their functions within the covered entity.

controls.title: Administrative requirements — safeguards
controls.description: A covered entity must have in place appropriate administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to protect the privacy of protected health information.

controls.title: Administrative requirements — complaints
controls.description: A covered entity must provide a process for individuals to make complaints concerning the covered entity's policies and procedures or its compliance with such policies and procedures or the requirements of the Privacy Rule.

controls.title: Administrative requirements — mitigation
controls.description: A covered entity must mitigate, to the extent practicable, any harmful effect that is known to the covered entity of a use or disclosure of protected health information in violation of its policies and procedures or the requirements of the Privacy Rule by the covered entity or its business associate.

controls.title: Administrative requirements — documentation
controls.description: A covered entity must maintain its privacy policies and procedures in written or electronic form, and if a communication, activity, or designation is required by the Privacy Rule to be documented, the covered entity must maintain such documentation for six years from the date of its creation or the date when it last was in effect, whichever is later.

controls.title: Definitions — breach
controls.description: A breach is the acquisition, access, use, or disclosure of protected health information in a manner not permitted under the Privacy Rule which compromises the security or privacy of the protected health information, unless the covered entity or business associate demonstrates a low probability that the protected health information has been compromised based on a risk assessment.

controls.title: Notification to individuals
controls.description: A covered entity shall, following the discovery of a breach of unsecured protected health information, notify each individual whose unsecured protected health information has been, or is reasonably believed by the covered entity to have been, accessed, acquired, used, or disclosed as a result of such breach.

controls.title: Timeliness of notification to individuals
controls.description: Notifications to individuals must be made without unreasonable delay and in no case later than 60 calendar days after the discovery of a breach.

controls.title: Content of notification to individuals
controls.description: The notification must include a brief description of what happened, the types of information involved, the steps individuals should take to protect themselves, what the covered entity is doing to investigate and mitigate, and contact procedures.

controls.title: Notification to the media
controls.description: For a breach of unsecured protected health information involving more than 500 residents of a State or jurisdiction, a covered entity shall notify prominent media outlets serving the State or jurisdiction without unreasonable delay and in no case later than 60 calendar days after discovery.

controls.title: Notification to the Secretary of HHS
controls.description: A covered entity shall notify the Secretary of breaches of unsecured protected health information. For breaches involving 500 or more individuals, notification must occur contemporaneously with individual notification. For breaches involving fewer than 500 individuals, notification may be made annually.

controls.title: Notification by a business associate
controls.description: A business associate shall, following the discovery of a breach of unsecured protected health information, notify the covered entity of such breach. Such notice shall include the identification of each individual whose unsecured protected health information has been, or is reasonably believed to have been, accessed, acquired, used, or disclosed during the breach.

controls.title: Law enforcement delay
controls.description: If a law enforcement official determines that a notification, notice, or posting required under the Breach Notification Rule would impede a criminal investigation or cause damage to national security, the covered entity or business associate shall delay notification for a period specified by the official.

controls.title: Administrative requirements — burden of proof
controls.description: In the event of a use or disclosure of protected health information in violation of the Privacy Rule, the covered entity or business associate has the burden of demonstrating that all notifications were made as required or that the use or disclosure did not constitute a breach.
